purpose: Replay-only contract for testing external Hugging Face image-to-image models against Twin Card source images before any production integration.
safetyRules: Do not use live participant photos with external providers unless consent and legal/commercial review explicitly allow it.
Do not add this provider to the production funnel until model license, privacy posture, retention behavior, and commercial rights are approved.
Use the same three replay source images for every candidate so marketing can compare model quality objectively.
Store external-provider outputs under twin-card-replay only; never overwrite participant generated/avatar or print artifacts.
defaultPromptSuffix: Transform the reference photo into a clean, polished 2D wellness avatar while preserving the same person's visible identity, face shape, hair, skin tone, eyewear, pose, framing, and natural expression. Keep it healthcare-friendly, premium, simple background, no text, no logos.
defaultNegativePrompt: different person, changed identity, changed face, changed hairstyle, different hair color, removed glasses, added glasses, different age, glam beauty filter, plastic skin, text, logo, watermark, distorted face, extra limbs

promptVariants.label: Baseline wellness avatar
promptVariants.promptSuffix: Transform the reference photo into a clean, polished 2D wellness avatar while preserving the same person's visible identity, face shape, hair, skin tone, eyewear, pose, framing, and natural expression. Keep it healthcare-friendly, premium, simple background, no text, no logos.
promptVariants.negativePrompt: different person, changed identity, changed face, changed hairstyle, different hair color, removed glasses, added glasses, different age, glam beauty filter, plastic skin, text, logo, watermark, distorted face, extra limbs

promptVariants.label: Identity lock
promptVariants.promptSuffix: Create a subtle 2D wellness avatar version of the reference photo. Keep the same person highly recognizable: same face geometry, eyes, nose, mouth, jawline, hair, skin tone, eyewear, expression, camera angle, pose, and crop. Make only gentle illustration cleanup and lighting polish. Do not beautify, age-shift, slim, reshape, or invent features. Simple premium healthcare background, no text, no logos.
promptVariants.negativePrompt: different person, low likeness, changed identity, changed face structure, changed eyes, changed nose, changed mouth, changed jawline, changed hairstyle, different hair color, removed glasses, added glasses, different age, beauty filter, glam makeup, plastic skin, face swap, text, logo, watermark, distorted face, extra limbs

promptVariants.label: Minimal avatar edit
promptVariants.promptSuffix: Keep the reference photo composition and identity almost unchanged, then convert it into a clean soft 2D avatar card portrait. Preserve the exact visible person, facial proportions, hair, eyewear, expression, pose, and framing. Apply light illustration styling only: smoother edges, warmer health-and-wellness color, clean background, premium approachable finish. No text, no logos.
promptVariants.negativePrompt: different person, changed identity, over-stylized, cartoon caricature, changed face, changed hair, changed expression, changed glasses, beauty retouch, glam filter, younger, older, text, logo, watermark, distorted face, extra limbs

modelRecipes.promptSuffix: Transform the reference photo into a clean, polished 2D wellness avatar while preserving the same person's visible identity, face shape, hair, skin tone, eyewear, pose, framing, and natural expression. Keep it healthcare-friendly, premium, simple background, no text, no logos.
modelRecipes.negativePrompt: different person, changed identity, changed face, changed hairstyle, different hair color, removed glasses, added glasses, different age, glam beauty filter, plastic skin, text, logo, watermark, distorted face, extra limbs
notes: Use for the primary candidate because it produced the best observed likeness in replay.
Prompt can tolerate more stylistic language than Qwen without producing text artifacts in the observed tests.

modelRecipes.purpose: Fallback image-edit recipe tuned to avoid Qwen text-rendering artifacts.
modelRecipes.prompt: Transform the reference photo into a clean, polished 2D wellness avatar while preserving the same person's visible identity, face, face features, face shape, hair, skin tone, pose, framing, and natural expression. Keep it healthcare-friendly, premium, simple background, no text, no logos.
modelRecipes.negativePrompt: text, letters, numbers, words, caption, label, logo, badge, sign, watermark, UI, typography, writing, different person, changed identity, changed face, changed hair, changed expression, changed glasses, beauty filter, glam retouch, distorted face, extra limbs
notes: Qwen-Image-Edit is documented as strong at text rendering and precise text editing, so this recipe avoids card/banner language and explicitly excludes text artifacts.
Keep the instruction short and edit-focused because Replicate's Qwen Image Edit schema exposes a prompt instruction for editing the given image.

billing.pricingSource: Hugging Face Inference Providers pricing and fal.ai FLUX.1 Kontext dev pricing
notes: Hugging Face Inference Providers charge the same rates as the selected provider with no additional Hugging Face fee.
fal.ai FLUX.1 Kontext dev is priced per generated output megapixel and images are rounded up to the nearest megapixel.
Failed provider calls are recorded with zero estimated successful output units unless an output image is returned.

topCandidateStrategy.selectionRule: Rank model/provider pairs, not only model ids, because provider routing changes availability, billing, and output behavior.

currentWinner.reason: Current preferred candidate after side-by-side replay. Nano Banana 2 Edit produced the strongest avatar output and has direct fal.ai audit headers plus pricing API support.
currentWinner.nextTest: Before comparing against GPT Image 2 Edit, keep fal response headers, pricing API results, and billing-events permission status in every replay manifest.

rankedCandidates.strength: Current preferred direct-provider image-edit candidate for likeness-preserving 2D wellness-avatar outputs.
rankedCandidates.why: The Qwen vs Nano Banana replay made Nano Banana 2 Edit the new front-runner. fal response headers expose request id and billable units, and fal Platform pricing gives current unit price.
rankedCandidates.knownIssue: Request-level billing events require a fal admin API key; with a normal inference key, cost visibility relies on response billable units multiplied by pricing API unit price.

rankedCandidates.strength: Next direct fal.ai comparison candidate because it has an edit endpoint and a different model family from Nano Banana.
rankedCandidates.why: Use this only after the fal audit path records price, request id, billable units, and billing-events permission status.
rankedCandidates.knownIssue: fal pricing reports a generic units price for this endpoint; final per-run cost must be validated through request headers and, ideally, admin billing events.

rankedCandidates.strength: Use the normalized raw source image in the card frame when external generation is unavailable or quality is unacceptable.
rankedCandidates.why: The raw-image fallback preserves identity and keeps the booth flow moving.
rankedCandidates.knownIssue: This is not an AI avatar output; use only as operational fallback.

researchBacklog.reason: Recommended next non-Qwen comparison candidate. It completed a one-card Hugging Face replay probe via replicate on 2026-05-26.
High-ranking different model family on the Hugging Face image-to-image list, but the 2026-05-26 replay probe could not find inference-provider information through the current Hugging Face route.
Potential newer Qwen edit candidate; verify provider availability, pricing, and license before ranking.
Test with the minimal_avatar and identity_lock prompt variants to see whether it can preserve facial features while adding a more illustrated look.
Best current model for controlled image edits; pair it with stricter prompt variants before trying LoRA/style-specific cartoon adapters.

candidateModels.reason: Best first FLUX-family image-editing candidate from Hugging Face for preserving a source image while applying a new visual direction.
Strong image-edit candidate for turning a source photo into a stylized avatar while retaining visible structure.
Newer Qwen image-edit variant worth comparing if hosted inference is available.
FLUX-family image-to-image candidate to compare against FLUX.1 Kontext if available.
